zh-hans.lifeSummary: James Lowe于1271年出生在英国德比夏爾摩利。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1303年在英国享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lowe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Low  .

German (Löwe) and Danish (Løwe): from Middle High German lēwe, löuwe ‘lion’, hence a nickname for a brave or regal person. In some cases the surname may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn distinguished by the sign of a lion. It is also found in Sweden. Compare Loewe 1, Lau , Lave , and Lurvey .
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Löwe): artificial name from German Löwe ‘lion’.

variants.name: Lau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wai, Wing, Kam, Kwok, Ming, Chi, Yuk, Chun, Kin, Ching, Kwan, Man.

German: nickname for a physically strong person, from Middle High German louwe, lauwe ‘lion’. In some cases the surname may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn distinguished by the sign of a lion. Compare Lowe .
German: topographic name for someone living in a bush-covered area or clearing, Middle Low German lo, loch, lage, Middle High German lō.

variants.name: Loew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Eckhard, Fritz, Hans, Heinz, Hermann, Manfred, Raimund.
German (mainly Löw): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2). The surname Loew is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Low 5.

variants.name: Lobe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Erwin, Horst, Otto, Winfried.
German (Löbe): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2).
German: metonymic occupational name for a tanner, from Middle High German lō ‘tanning bark’.

variants.name: Loewe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Armin, Gerhard, Helmuth, Klaus, Kurt.
German (mainly Löwe): see Lowe 2.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Löwe): artificial name from German Löwe ‘lion’. Compare Lowe 3.

variants.name: Loewen
meanings: German (Löwen): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2). Compare Lowen 2.

variants.name: Low
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived near a tumulus, mound or hill, Middle English lowe, from Old English hlāw (see Law 2).

Scottish and English: nickname for a short man, from Middle English lah, lowe (Old Norse lágr; the word was adopted first into the northern dialects of Middle English, where Scandinavian influence was strong, and then spread south, with regular alteration of the vowel quality).

English and Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname for a violent or dangerous person, from Anglo-Norman French lou, leu ‘wolf’ (from Latin lupus). Wolves were relatively common in Britain at the time when most surnames were formed, as there still existed large tracts of uncleared forest.

variants.name: Lurvey
meanings: Americanized form of German Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2).
History: Peter Lurvy, the immigrant ancestor of this family, was in Ipswich, MA, by 1679.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: James Lowe was born in 1271, in Morley, Derbyshire, England. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1303, in England, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: James Lowe est né en 1271, à Morley, Derbyshire, Angleterre. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1303, à Angleterre, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: James Lowe wurde 1271 in Morley, Derbyshire, England geboren. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1303 im Alter von 32 Jahren in England.

it.lifeSummary: James Lowe è nato il nel 1271 nel Morley, Derbyshire, England. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1303, nel England, all'età di 32 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: James Lowe(은)는 1271년 영국 더비실르 모를리에서 태어났다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1303년 영국에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: James Lowe nasceu em 1271, em Morley, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1303, em Inglaterra, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: James Lowe родился в 1271 г. в местности морли, дирбишир, Англия. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1303 г. в местности Англия в возрасте 32 лет.

es.lifeSummary: James Lowe nació en 1271, en Morley, Derbyshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1303, en Inglaterra, a la edad de 32 años.
